summary_one_line: Input must contain the literal 'Content-Type:' header so search_header sets customct non-NULL and the true branch is taken

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must embed the literal ASCII string 'Content-Type:' (and an enclosing MIME/userheaders structure) so that search_header(part->userheaders, STRCONST("Content-Type")) returns non-NULL, making customct non-NULL at line 1792 and taking the true branch (contenttype = customct).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the exact byte run 'Content-Type:' starting at offset 0x24 (47 43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 65 3a = 'GContent-Type:'); the per-offset byte-set shows W concentrated on these literal bytes (e.g. 0x24=47('G'), 0x25=43('C'), 0x26=6f('o') with x20 counts), matching the STRCONST("Content-Type") operand the comparison interception logs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds lack the 'Content-Type:' literal at those offsets (e.g. naive seeds carry runs of 2d('-') and 00 bytes; offset 0x25 L bytes are 80/00/41/74 not 43('C')), so search_header finds no matching header, customct stays NULL, and the false branch is taken (L1792 T=0 F=41).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer recording the STRCONST("Content-Type") string-CMP operand and substitutes that literal into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the header string that naive/value_profile can only stumble on by chance; value_profile_cmplog wins over value_profile by the same I2S mutator (the only delta between vp and vpc is I2SRandReplace).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of if(customct) at mime.c:1792, where customct is set by search_header for a Content-Type header.
The globally blocked side is the true branch at line 1792.
Winner-resolving seeds contain the literal Content-Type header string.
At offset 0x25 the winner bytes are concentrated on 43 ('C') across all 20 occurrences, while the loser bytes are not.
The winner takes the true side at the blocker while the loser takes only the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1792  T=20 F=21  T=0 F=41  if(customct)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets, and value_profile lacks it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, isolating I2S as the technique delta for the vpc>vp pair.
Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique with prob_div=1.00.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 272 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed acb98175a6d3d5c3` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 10 and 11 of the 12-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on search_header("Content-Type") where cmplog/vpc reach the true branch no faster than naive/value_profile (no I2S advantage), or where winner seeds resolve the branch without the literal 'Content-Type:' bytes present.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the structural framing around the literal (userheaders parsing / null-terminator placement) is also required, or whether the bare 'Content-Type:' substring suffices, is not fully isolated by the byte-diff alone.
